0_Overview: What is Java: Object-oriented, Platform-independent, Compiled to Bytecode
Benefits: Write Once, Run Anywhere (JVM)
Benefits: Mature Ecosystem — Spring, Hibernate, Maven, Gradle
Benefits: Strong Typing and Compile-time Safety
Benefits: Excellent Performance with JIT Compilation
Benefits: Massive Community and Enterprise Adoption
Benefits: Backward Compatibility — Old Code Still Runs
Benefits: Rich Standard Library
Drawbacks: Verbose Compared to Kotlin, Python
Drawbacks: Slow Startup Time (improving with GraalVM Native Image)
Drawbacks: High Memory Usage
Drawbacks: Boilerplate: Getters, Setters, Constructors (use Lombok or Records)
When to Use: Enterprise Applications
When to Use: Android Development (though Kotlin is preferred)
When to Use: Large-scale Backend Systems
When to Use: Microservices with Spring Boot
When NOT to Use: Scripting, Data Science, Mobile (iOS), Frontend
Java vs Kotlin: Verbosity, Null Safety, Coroutines
Java vs C#: Platform, Ecosystem
Java vs Go: Startup Time, Memory, Simplicity
Java LTS Versions: 8, 11, 17, 21 — Key Features
JVM Languages: Kotlin, Scala, Groovy, Clojure
1_Java_Basics: Java Program Structure: package, import, class, main method
Primitive Types: byte, short, int, long, float, double, char, boolean
Wrapper Classes: Integer, Long, Double, Boolean, Character
String Methods: length, charAt, substring, indexOf, contains, replace, split, trim
Control Flow: if-else, switch (traditional and enhanced), for, while, do-while
Enhanced for Loop: for (Type item : collection)
Math Class: abs, max, min, pow, sqrt, random
Java 14+ Records: record Point(int x, int y) {}
2_OOP: Access Modifiers: public, private, protected, package-private
Interfaces: default methods, static methods, private methods (Java 9+)
static: Members, Initializer Blocks, Nested Classes
Inner Classes: Static Nested, Inner, Local, Anonymous
Enums: Fields, Methods, Constructors, EnumSet, EnumMap
Object Class Methods: equals, hashCode, toString, clone, finalize
3_Collections_Framework: Map: HashMap, LinkedHashMap, TreeMap, Hashtable, ConcurrentHashMap
Deque: ArrayDeque as Stack and Queue
Collections Utility Class: sort, shuffle, reverse, min, max, frequency
Arrays Utility Class: sort, binarySearch, fill, copyOf, asList
4_Generics_and_Functional: Generics: Type Parameters <T>, Bounded Types <T extends Number>
Functional Interfaces: Predicate, Function, Consumer, Supplier, BiFunction
Stream Intermediate: filter, map, flatMap, distinct, sorted, peek, limit, skip
Stream Terminal: collect, forEach, reduce, count, min, max, anyMatch, allMatch, findFirst
Collectors: toList, toSet, toMap, groupingBy, partitioningBy, joining
Optional<T>: isPresent, get, orElse, orElseGet, map, flatMap, filter
5_Concurrency: Thread Lifecycle: NEW, RUNNABLE, BLOCKED, WAITING, TIMED_WAITING, TERMINATED
CompletableFuture: thenApply, thenCompose, thenCombine, exceptionally, allOf
6_Spring_Boot: Spring Core: IoC Container, Dependency Injection, ApplicationContext
ResponseEntity<T> for Full HTTP Response Control
Spring Security: JWT, OAuth2 Resource Server
Spring Boot Actuator: Health, Metrics, Info Endpoints
7_Java_Modern_Features: Java 8: Lambdas, Streams, Optional, Default Methods, Date/Time API
Java 9: Modules (JPMS), JShell, Collection Factory Methods
Java 11: String Methods, Files.readString, HTTP Client API
Java 17: Sealed Classes, Pattern Matching switch (Preview)
Java 21: Virtual Threads, Sequenced Collections, Record Patterns
GraalVM Native Image: Fast Startup, Low Memory
8_Java_Ecosystem: Gradle: Groovy DSL vs Kotlin DSL
Testcontainers: Integration Tests with Real Databases
Hibernate: ORM, HQL, Criteria API, N+1 Problem
Resilience4j: Circuit Breaker, Retry, Rate Limiter
